description: Executable component registry for genui_pipeline_v1 step_4. Each entry is a JSON spec (NOT markdown). Only component_type names listed here are valid for the component_planner — LLM must not invent new ones. Vocabulary aligned with figma-refs/orchestration_rules.json (fromType/toType).
source_note: v0 extracted from genui.html `templates` object. layout_spec/tokens are best-effort defaults; tokens map to DESIGN.md semantic tokens (to be reconciled).

categories.chrome: Persistent system chrome (status bar, app bar, bottom nav, tab bar).
categories.container: Surface that wraps content (card, list-item, dialog, snackbar).
categories.overlay: Quick Settings, notification shade, system dialog.
categories.navigation: Pill tab, bottom nav, tab bar.

btn-contained.description: Filled rectangular button, 48dp min height, 16dp radius, primary-color background with centered label-md text.
btn-contained.purpose: Primary CTA emphasizing the most important action on a complex screen — at most one per content area.
use_when: the action is the top priority on the screen
the button competes with other text/images and needs visual weight
dont_use_when: a flat button on a toolbar/dialog already provides the same action
more than one primary action is needed in the same area

btn-outlined.description: Transparent rectangular button with a 16dp-radius outline border and centered label-md text, 48dp min height.
btn-outlined.purpose: Secondary action paired with a contained primary — acknowledges importance without dominating the layout.
use_when: presenting a secondary choice next to a primary CTA
both options need to feel selectable but not visually dominant
dont_use_when: the action is the most important on the screen — use btn-contained
the action lives in a toolbar or dialog footer — use btn-flat

btn-flat.description: Transparent text-only button, 48dp min height with 12dp-radius pressed state — no fill, no border.
btn-flat.purpose: Lightweight action inside toolbars, dialog footers and dense surfaces where added layers would create visual noise.
use_when: action lives inside an app bar, dialog, or list-item footer
the screen already has enough visual structure
dont_use_when: the action needs strong visual emphasis — use btn-contained

fab.description: Circular pill-radius button, 56x56 dp, primary-color fill containing a single icon — typically anchored bottom-right.
fab.purpose: Persistent shortcut to the screen's main creation/composition action that must remain reachable while content scrolls.
use_when: a single primary action needs to stay reachable across long scrollable content
in glanceable or minimal-touch contexts where space for text is limited
dont_use_when: more than one action needs equal emphasis
the action is contextual to a list item rather than the screen

switch.description: Pill-shaped track with a sliding thumb that moves left (off) or right (on); track tints to primary color when on.
switch.purpose: Toggle a single boolean setting that takes effect immediately — no Save needed.
use_when: a setting is binary and applies immediately
appearing in a settings list as a row trailing control
dont_use_when: the choice needs to be confirmed or saved later — use a checkbox
more than two states are needed

checkbox.description: Square 4dp-radius box with a checkmark when on; an optional inline text label sits to the right.
checkbox.purpose: Select zero, one or many items from a list — also expresses indeterminate state in select-all rows.
use_when: users may pick multiple options
Edit/Select mode header offering 'Select all' affordance
dont_use_when: the choice is mutually exclusive — use radio
the action takes effect instantly with no save step — use switch

radio.description: Circular outline with a filled inner dot when on; an optional inline text label sits to the right.
radio.purpose: Pick exactly one option from a small mutually-exclusive set — typical for visible groups of 2–6 choices.
use_when: the choice is mutually exclusive and the full set should stay visible
presenting a settings choice (sort by, theme, etc.)
dont_use_when: multiple selections are allowed — use checkbox
the set has many options — use a dropdown or list

chip.description: Pill-shaped 32dp tall surface-2 container with label-sm text and optional leading icon, tints to primary when selected.
chip.purpose: Compact filter or quick-select control — typically arranged in a horizontal scrolling row.
use_when: filtering, suggesting, or representing tags
the user needs quick toggle of contextual attributes
dont_use_when: the choice is a primary/destination action — use a button
the set is exclusive radio-style — use radio

input.description: 12dp-radius surface-2 text field, 48dp tall, with optional leading/trailing icon and a label that floats above on focus.
input.purpose: Single-line free-text entry for forms — names, emails, numeric values, search queries within a flow.
use_when: the user types a value the system can't predict
forms, sign-up, settings entry
dont_use_when: the input is a query intended to filter content — use search
the value is a binary or chosen option — use switch / radio

search.description: Pill-shaped 48dp text field with leading magnifier icon — shows recent suggestions and auto-complete on focus.
search.purpose: Help users find content fast across large data sets — pinned at the top of list/browse surfaces.
use_when: the screen has a lot of items and finding one specific entry is the primary task
exposing recent terms / suggestions adds value
dont_use_when: no searchable corpus exists — use input
all items fit on screen at once and filtering chips are enough

status-bar.description: Translucent 28dp-tall strip at the top of every screen showing time, signal, battery and notification icons in label-xs text.
status-bar.purpose: System chrome — informs the user about device state across all contexts (lock, home, app).
use_when: any full-screen surface that owns the top edge
dont_use_when: the surface is presented as a modal/sheet underneath the system status — already handled by host

appbar.description: 56dp tall transparent bar with optional back icon, a title-md title, and up to three trailing action icons.
appbar.purpose: Identify the current screen and surface up to three priority actions plus a back/menu affordance.
use_when: every app screen — shows where the user is and the available global actions
screens that need a back button or section title
dont_use_when: very compact dialogs/sheets where a header alone suffices
full-bleed media views — hide the chrome instead

bottomnav.description: 56dp tall surface-2 bar at the bottom holding 3–5 navigation destinations as icon + label-xs cells.
bottomnav.purpose: Top-level destination switching across the app's primary sections — always visible, single-tap reach.
use_when: the app has 3–5 peer destinations the user moves between often
dont_use_when: the app has only one destination
navigation is hierarchical (deep drill-down) — use back button + tab-bar

pill-tab.description: Pill-shaped 56dp tall surface-2 segmented control floating near the bottom — typically 2–4 cells with selected one filled.
pill-tab.purpose: Compact floating navigation between a small set of peer modes (e.g., List/Grid, Day/Week/Month).
use_when: switching between a tiny set of view modes within a single screen
the screen is glanceable / minimal-touch and a full bottomnav would be too heavy
dont_use_when: the app needs to switch between top-level destinations — use bottomnav

tab-bar.description: Horizontal row of 48dp text-label tabs on a transparent track with a moving indicator under the active label.
tab-bar.purpose: Switch between sub-categories of one section (Subtab pattern) — fixed if 4 or fewer, scrollable beyond.
use_when: splitting a section into 2–N peer sub-categories
subtabs sit beneath the app bar / search field
dont_use_when: navigation is between top-level destinations — use bottomnav
only 2 modes — pill-tab feels lighter

card.description: 22dp-radius surface-2 container, 16dp padding, holds a title-sm header, optional body-sm value, icon and actions.
card.purpose: Generic content container for grouping related text/actions when no more specialized card fits.
use_when: grouping related title + body + actions on a content surface
no more specialized card (eta_card, weather_glance_card…) applies
dont_use_when: showing a list — use list-item
showing a glanceable summary — use the typed *_summary_card variants

list-item.description: Transparent 56dp tall row with leading icon, body-md title, optional secondary text on the right and trailing actions.
list-item.purpose: Single entry in a vertically scrolling list — selectable, swipable, with consistent vertical rhythm across the list.
use_when: rendering each item in a list (settings, contacts, messages, files)
items follow the same shape and could be many
dont_use_when: items have heterogeneous shapes — use card
the row needs a strong selection indicator and is part of a small set — use card.radio

dialog.description: 28dp-radius modal surface (~280dp wide), title-md heading and body-md description with right-aligned action buttons.
dialog.purpose: Block the user to confirm a decision (delete, sign out) or display blocking content (date picker, chooser).
use_when: the user must confirm a destructive or important action
a flow needs structured input (date/time/color picker)
dont_use_when: the message is informational and dismissible — use snackbar / action toast
removing trivially-restorable data — skip the confirm

snackbar.description: 16dp-radius surface-3 pill, 48dp tall, body-sm message left, optional action label right — auto-dismisses after a few seconds.
snackbar.purpose: Confirm a system-initiated event with optional one-tap follow-up (e.g., '1 file downloaded — Open file').
use_when: informing about a completed action with a meaningful follow-up
the message is non-blocking and timely
dont_use_when: the user must acknowledge — use dialog
providing dismiss-only buttons (Close, OK, Done) — One UI guideline forbids this

divider.description: Single-pixel horizontal hairline using the divider token color, full available width.
divider.purpose: Visually separate adjacent rows or sections that share a surface.
use_when: between list items that lack contrast on their own
between subgroups inside a card.stacked-group
dont_use_when: rows already have card backgrounds — no extra divider needed

badge.description: Tiny pill (>=16dp), error-colored fill with label-xs count text — typically attached to an icon's top-right.
badge.purpose: Indicate unread/unseen counts or status alerts on icons in app bars, tabs and home-screen icons.
use_when: counts of unread items, alerts, available updates
small textual badges (numbers, '!' or single short labels)
dont_use_when: long strings — use a chip

now-bar.description: Generic pill-shaped 56dp glass widget anchored above the gesture bar — leading icon, label-md title, optional trailing actions.
now-bar.purpose: Persistent contextual now-bar slot — default container when no specialized variant (media, charging, single-line) applies.
use_when: showing live state from an app the user is engaged with
the surface is lock/home/glanceable/driving
dont_use_when: showing media playback — use now-bar.media-player
showing charging — use now-bar.charging

qs-toggle.description: Square 64x64dp tile, 14dp radius, surface-2 background — icon + label-xs label, tints to primary when on.
qs-toggle.purpose: Single binary toggle inside the Quick Settings panel (Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, DND).
use_when: a binary system feature needs one-tap toggle from QS
tile arranged in a grid with peers
dont_use_when: the value isn't binary — use slider or detail screen
outside Quick Settings — use switch

qs-grid.description: Transparent grid container (~360x220dp) holding 6–10 qs-toggle tiles in a 4×N layout inside the Quick Settings shade.
qs-grid.purpose: The matrix that hosts and arranges all qs-toggle tiles in the Quick Settings panel.
use_when: modeling the expanded Quick Settings shade with multiple toggles
dont_use_when: showing just one toggle inline — use qs-toggle directly

media-card.description: 22dp-radius surface-2 card (~280x140dp) with album art + title + subtitle stacked over a row of transport controls.
media-card.purpose: Full media preview with playback controls — typical of the Quick Settings media tile and lock-screen widget.
use_when: media is currently active and the user wants quick transport control
Quick Settings or glanceable surfaces
dont_use_when: showing the now-bar — use now-bar.media-player
playback context belongs in an immersive screen — use media-player.tile

typical_content.guidance: label = track / show / playlist title (the foreground item). value = artist · album, or service · episode/duration. If casting video, mention the platform. Always real-feeling media metadata.

notification-card.description: 18dp-radius glass card with app icon, title and one-line preview — expands to show full content + actions.
notification-card.purpose: Standard heads-up / shade notification — surface an app event the user should know about.
use_when: delivering a routine app notification (message, calendar, reminder)
in lock or notification-shade contexts
dont_use_when: live/ongoing event — use notification.live
AI-generated content — use notification.ai-regular

examples.label: Messages · Mom
examples.value: Don't forget dinner at 7 — call when you're close

examples.label: Calendar
examples.value: Design review starts in 10 min · Conf Rm 4

examples.label: Coupang · Delivery
examples.value: Your package was left at the door

typical_content.guidance: label = app name + optional sub-source (e.g. 'Messages · Mom', 'Calendar'). value = a short one-line preview of the event content. Realistic copy — include names, amounts, places.

widget-small.description: Square 96x96dp glass tile, 16dp radius, with optional leading icon and a prominent value (title-sm) — fits home/lock 1×1 grid cell.
widget-small.purpose: Glanceable mini-widget for one piece of information (today's steps, calendar count, weather temp).
use_when: home/lock screen needs a single value at a glance
the metric makes sense without context
dont_use_when: the metric needs richer context — use a typed *_summary_card
no value is available — hide the widget

keyboard.description: Full-width software keyboard, ~260dp tall, surface-3 background — slides up from the bottom when an input is focused.
keyboard.purpose: OS-managed text entry surface — the registry references it so layouts can budget vertical space when fields are focused.
use_when: modeling screens that show a focused text field
reserving content area above the keyboard in edit mode
dont_use_when: no input is focused — the keyboard is hidden

eta_card._status: planned — not yet rendered by genui.html
eta_card.description: Compact card with a small label-sm caption and a large headline-md ETA value plus optional route icon.
eta_card.purpose: Glanceable arrival-time read-out for navigation, ride-share, transit — readable at speed.
use_when: driving / minimal-touch contexts that need a one-glance ETA
showing arrival time with no extra interaction
dont_use_when: the user needs full route control — use navigation_turn_card or open the maps app

typical_content.guidance: label = category + short context (e.g. 'ETA · Home', 'Driver arriving'). value = arrival time + secondary fact (traffic, stops, driver name, status). Always concrete numbers.

message_summary_card._status: planned — not yet rendered by genui.html
message_summary_card.description: Compact card with sender label-sm and a body-md preview of the latest message — taps into the conversation.
message_summary_card.purpose: Glanceable preview of an unread/recent message that lets the user open or reply quickly.
use_when: showing the most recent unread message in a glanceable summary
driving / quick-view contexts
dont_use_when: showing a list of messages — use list-item rows in an inbox

examples.label: Mom
examples.value: Don't forget dinner at 7 — call when you're close

examples.label: Slack · #design
examples.value: Eun-bi: Pushed the new tokens, can you review?

examples.label: Email · Stripe
examples.value: Your receipt for $42.18 — thanks for using Stripe

typical_content.guidance: label = sender name + optional channel/count (e.g. 'Mom', 'Slack · #design', 'Sora (3 new)'). value = a single-line message preview, written like a real text — keep punctuation and personality.

media_control_bar.description: Pill-shaped 64dp tall row with album thumb, label-md title and a 3-button transport (prev / play-pause / next).
media_control_bar.purpose: Compact transport control for active media when the surface can't accommodate full art (driving, glanceable).
use_when: driving / minimal-touch / quick-view surfaces with active media
space is too tight for media-card
dont_use_when: no media is playing — hide
rich preview is preferred — use media-card

typical_content.guidance: label = track / episode / station name (the foreground item). value = artist · album, or show · episode + secondary detail (time remaining, source). Always real-feeling music/podcast metadata.

action_chip_row.description: Horizontal pill-row container, ~44dp tall, holds 2–5 action chips on a surface-2 backplate.
action_chip_row.purpose: Group of low-emphasis quick actions tied to the surrounding card or content.
use_when: offering 2–5 contextual quick actions under a piece of content
actions should feel like equally-weighted siblings
dont_use_when: any action should dominate — promote it to a button
actions need persistent screen-level placement — use bottom nav or app bar

examples.label: Quick replies
examples.value: Yes, please / On my way / Call you back

typical_content.guidance: value contains comma- or slash-separated chip labels (3–5 chips). label is the row's purpose. Chips should be short verbs/nouns, not full sentences.

calendar_summary_card.description: Card with a label-sm date/header and a title-md value showing the next event title and time.
calendar_summary_card.purpose: Glanceable summary of the user's next calendar event — what and when, no further interaction.
use_when: home / lock / quick-view glanceable summary of upcoming events
dont_use_when: no upcoming event — hide the card
the user needs to manage events — use the calendar app

examples.label: Tomorrow
examples.value: Flight to SFO · 7:45 AM · Terminal B

typical_content.guidance: label = when (e.g. 'Next up · Today', 'Tomorrow', 'In 30 min'). value = event title + time + optional location. Time first when imminent, location when relevant.

weather_glance_card.description: Card with weather-condition icon, label-sm location/condition and a large headline-md temperature.
weather_glance_card.purpose: Glanceable current temperature + condition for the user's location.
use_when: home / lock / quick-view summary of current weather
dont_use_when: the user needs the full forecast — open the weather app

typical_content.guidance: label = location + condition phrase (e.g. 'Seoul · Partly cloudy'). value = temperature + secondary fact (feels-like, high/low, when rain starts, UV). Use real city names.

navigation_turn_card.description: High-emphasis primary-color card showing turn arrow icon, label-sm distance and a headline-md instruction.
navigation_turn_card.purpose: Driving directions card — the next maneuver shown with maximum legibility, hands-free contexts.
use_when: active turn-by-turn navigation
driving / minimal-touch contexts that need the next instruction prominent
dont_use_when: navigation isn't active — use eta_card
the user needs full route detail — open the maps app

typical_content.guidance: label = distance to maneuver (e.g. 'In 400 m', 'Now', 'In 200 ft'). value = imperative direction with street/landmark name. Use units consistent with the locale (m/km vs ft/mi).

reminder_card.description: Card with reminder icon, label-sm time/due and a body-md task summary — interactive, opens the reminder.
reminder_card.purpose: Glanceable reminder/to-do summary that lets the user mark complete or open the full task.
use_when: showing the next due reminder on home / lock / quick-view
dont_use_when: no active reminders — hide
showing a list of reminders — use list-item rows

examples.label: Today · 6:00 PM
examples.value: Pick up dry cleaning on the way home

typical_content.guidance: label = when it fires (time, relative time, or trigger phrase). value = the task as a short imperative sentence. Keep it personal and specific.

quick_toggle_row.description: Row of compact 16dp-radius toggle tiles on surface-2, each tile labelled with a tiny icon and label-xs caption.
quick_toggle_row.purpose: Bundle 3–6 fast on/off toggles (Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Do Not Disturb…) into a single quick-settings strip.
use_when: presenting a horizontal cluster of binary system toggles
embedded in quick-settings or focused panels
dont_use_when: toggles need detail / configuration — use card.toggle-row
only one toggle is needed — use switch

examples.label: Connectivity
examples.value: Wi-Fi · Bluetooth · Mobile data · Airplane mode

examples.label: Focus
examples.value: Do Not Disturb · Silent · Vibrate · Bedtime

examples.label: Display
examples.value: Dark mode · Auto-brightness · Blue-light filter · Rotation lock

examples.label: Driving
examples.value: Driving mode · Auto-reply · Voice only · Hotspot

examples.label: Cooking mode
examples.value: Screen on · Voice control · Hands-free · Big text

typical_content.guidance: label = toggle group purpose. value = 3–6 short toggle names separated by ' · ' (each tile shows one). Toggles are binary, so names should be nouns/states, not actions.

input_summary_card.description: 12dp-radius card, 64dp tall on surface-2, with a small label and a body-md value plus optional icon — looks like a populated input.
input_summary_card.purpose: Summary read-out of an already-entered field that opens the editor on tap (e.g., date picker preview, filter applied).
use_when: showing the current state of a structured input that opens its own editor
in glanceable summary screens where edits happen in a sub-flow
dont_use_when: the field is editable in place — use input

examples.label: Step 3 of 8
examples.value: Sauté garlic for 2 min until fragrant

typical_content.guidance: Used as a 'summary read-out' card — label is the field name, step, or progress; value is the populated content. Always concrete (real names, real durations, real values), never generic placeholders. If the field is empty, do not render this component.

status-bar.default.description: Full-width 35dp One UI status bar with bold time on the left and signal/wifi/battery icons on the right.
status-bar.default.purpose: Reference instance of the OS status bar used inside Figma layouts when building any full-screen Samsung surface.
use_when: modelling lock, home, app, quick-settings or notification-shade
layouts need to budget the top safe area
dont_use_when: live activity is active — use status-bar with live-activity state

status-bar.live-activity-chip.description: Compact 12dp-radius dark-glass pill (~76x24dp) with mini icon and 12sp label — embedded in the status bar.
status-bar.live-activity-chip.purpose: Persistent status indicator inside the status bar for live activities (timer, recording, navigation).
use_when: an ongoing system event needs always-visible micro status
the user should be able to tap to expand
dont_use_when: status fits a label-only badge — use badge
richer content needed in the shade — use notification.live

quick-settings.icons-row.description: Header strip (~408x40dp) of the expanded quick-settings panel showing date, profile and the small status icons row.
quick-settings.icons-row.purpose: Anchor the top of the quick-settings panel — provides date/identity and a slot for system shortcuts.
use_when: modeling the expanded quick-settings shade
dont_use_when: outside the quick-settings shade

single-toggle.half-toggle.description: Wide rounded tile (~199x88dp), 50dp radius on translucent grey, with bold title + small caption — fills half a quick-settings row.
single-toggle.half-toggle.purpose: Quick-settings toggle that needs both an icon AND a status caption (e.g., Wi-Fi name, Bluetooth device).
use_when: the toggle's value is more useful with status detail visible
in the expanded quick-settings panel
dont_use_when: the toggle is purely binary with no status — use single-toggle.single-toggle

single-toggle.single-toggle.description: Square 88x88dp icon-only tile, 50dp radius on translucent grey — used in the collapsed quick-settings strip.
single-toggle.single-toggle.purpose: Compact icon-only toggle for the collapsed/condensed quick-settings row where space is tight.
use_when: the collapsed quick-settings strip beneath the status bar
the toggle is widely understood from icon alone
dont_use_when: the user needs status text — use single-toggle.half-toggle

single-toggle.half-shortcut.description: Wide rounded tile (~199x88dp), 50dp radius, with title + caption — same footprint as half-toggle but launches a flow instead of toggling.
single-toggle.half-shortcut.purpose: Quick-settings shortcut that opens a deeper screen (Edit, Devices, Modes) rather than flipping a switch.
use_when: exposing a frequently-needed deep entry point in quick-settings
the action is navigation, not a toggle
dont_use_when: the action is a binary toggle — use single-toggle.half-toggle

quick-settings.slider-vertical.description: Tall vertical pill (~88x300dp), 44dp radius, translucent grey track that fills upward as the value grows.
quick-settings.slider-vertical.purpose: Adjust brightness, volume or media playback level inside the quick-settings panel with instant feedback.
use_when: the user changes a continuous value (brightness, volume) within Quick Settings
real-time feedback (audible/visual) is part of the interaction
dont_use_when: the value is a small set of choices — use radio or chip
outside the quick-settings layout — use the standard horizontal slider

quick-settings.smartthings-card.description: Wide 50dp-radius rolled-up tile (~408x86dp) on translucent grey, summarizing connected SmartThings devices.
quick-settings.smartthings-card.purpose: Single entry point in quick-settings that rolls multiple smart-home devices into a glanceable summary.
use_when: the user has SmartThings devices and wants quick control without opening the full app
dont_use_when: no smart devices are connected — hide entirely

now-bar.media-player.description: Pill-shaped 64dp glass bar (~248dp wide) with album art thumbnail, title, artist, and play/pause controls.
now-bar.media-player.purpose: Persistent media controls on lock/home screens — quick playback access without unlocking.
use_when: media is currently playing or paused
dont_use_when: no active media — show another now-bar variant or hide

typical_content.guidance: label = currently playing track / show title. value = artist or service + secondary detail (live, time remaining, chapter). Keep it short — the now-bar is narrow.

now-bar.dual-line.description: Pill-shaped 64dp glass now-bar showing two stacked text lines: bold title and lighter status — leading icon and trailing action.
now-bar.dual-line.purpose: Generic two-line now-bar slot for activities that need both a label and a status (timer, ride, transit).
use_when: the activity needs both a name and a status string
dont_use_when: only one short string is needed — use now-bar.single-line
the activity is media playback — use now-bar.media-player

now-bar.single-line.description: Pill-shaped 64dp glass now-bar with one large 26sp value and a leading icon — emphasized for headline state.
now-bar.single-line.purpose: Headline now-bar slot for a single big number/state (timer countdown, score, distance).
use_when: the activity is best represented by one prominent value
dont_use_when: additional context is essential — use now-bar.dual-line

now-bar.charging.description: Pill-shaped 64dp now-bar with a deep teal-blue alert tint, lightning icon and percentage + ETA strings.
now-bar.charging.purpose: Confirm the device started charging — distinguished color signals an alert-level state change.
use_when: device just started charging or charge state is notable
user benefits from a brief tinted confirmation
dont_use_when: battery is steady — use lock-screen.widget-battery instead

lock-screen.clock.description: Massive 112sp Samsung-Sans clock display, two-line stacked HH/MM, fully transparent backdrop — fills 412x164dp lock area.
lock-screen.clock.purpose: Anchor the lock screen — primary time read-out users glance at when waking the device.
use_when: lock screen — primary time display
dont_use_when: any non-lock context — use the status-bar time instead

lock-screen.weather-date.description: Single 14sp transparent text line just below the lock clock combining weather icon + temp and date string.
lock-screen.weather-date.purpose: Provide contextual day/weather info adjacent to the lock-screen clock at a glance.
use_when: the user benefits from the time + date + weather strip on the lock screen
dont_use_when: weather data is unavailable — degrade to date only

lock-screen.widget-battery.description: Small 138x62dp glass tile, 20dp radius, with battery icon, percentage title and short status caption.
lock-screen.widget-battery.purpose: Glanceable battery summary widget pinned in the lock-screen widget row.
use_when: lock-screen widget row when battery is the priority metric
dont_use_when: device just started charging — promote to now-bar.charging

lock-screen.widget-activity.description: Small 138x62dp glass tile, 20dp radius, with activity icon, count title (steps/calories) and unit caption.
lock-screen.widget-activity.purpose: Glanceable activity/health summary widget pinned in the lock-screen widget row.
use_when: lock-screen widget row showing today's activity progress
dont_use_when: no activity data — hide the slot

lock-screen.shortcut-circle.description: 47dp circular icon button on translucent dark glass, fully rounded — sits in the lock-screen bottom corners.
lock-screen.shortcut-circle.purpose: One-tap launcher for two key apps (typically Phone, Camera or Flashlight) directly from a locked device.
use_when: exposing exactly one or two priority shortcuts on the lock screen
dont_use_when: outside the lock context — use fab or a regular button

notification.ai-regular.description: 32dp-radius wide card on a blue-to-mint diagonal gradient — branded as a Galaxy AI generated notification.
notification.ai-regular.purpose: Surface a Galaxy AI suggestion or summary in the notification shade, distinguished by gradient.
use_when: the notification content is AI-generated (summaries, smart replies)
users should recognize the suggestion as AI-driven
dont_use_when: the notification originates from a user/app event — use notification-card

examples.label: Galaxy AI · Inbox summary
examples.value: 3 unread: 1 about Friday's dinner, 2 work follow-ups

examples.label: Smart reply suggestion
examples.value: Want me to draft 'Sounds good, see you at 7'?

examples.label: Routine ready
examples.value: It's 10 PM — start your wind-down? Lights, Do Not Disturb on

examples.label: Trip recap
examples.value: You took 47 photos in Busan — make a highlight reel?

examples.label: Meeting prep
examples.value: Design review in 30 min — here's a 2-line summary of last week's notes

typical_content.guidance: label = 'Galaxy AI · <topic>' or a short AI capability tag (e.g. 'Smart reply suggestion'). value = the AI's actual offer or summary, written in a helpful first-person voice. Always frame it as a suggestion or read-out, not a command.

notification.live.description: 32dp-radius translucent dark glass notification with progress/status display (timer, ride, navigation).
notification.live.purpose: Persistent live activity surfaced in the notification shade — represents an in-progress event the user is tracking.
use_when: ongoing events the user is actively monitoring (timer, ride, delivery)
the activity has time-relevant state changes
dont_use_when: completed or one-shot notifications — use notification-card
compact status only — use status-bar live-activity

notification.stack.description: 32dp-radius translucent dark card showing 'N more from this app' with peek of stacked notifications behind.
notification.stack.purpose: Collapse multiple notifications from the same app into one entry to keep the shade scannable.
use_when: an app has produced 3+ notifications close in time
stacking improves scannability of the shade
dont_use_when: only one or two notifications exist — show them individually

media-player.tile.description: 36dp-radius tile (~400x180dp) using album art as the full background, with overlaid title, artist and transport row.
media-player.tile.purpose: Immersive media player tile in the notification shade or media app — visual content takes priority.
use_when: presenting playing media with a strong visual identity (album cover, video preview)
shade or quick-settings expanded media tile
dont_use_when: no album art available — use media-card
extreme glanceability is needed — use media_control_bar

notifications.section-label.description: Single text label (14sp) on transparent ground — typographic section divider above grouped notification cards.
notifications.section-label.purpose: Group notifications by source/category in the shade ('Today', 'Older', 'Silent').
use_when: the shade needs categorical separation between notification groups
dont_use_when: only a few notifications exist and grouping adds no value

dialog.website-share-header.description: 24dp-radius dark glass header (380x78dp) with site title and small URL, leading favicon — dressed for share-target dialogs.
dialog.website-share-header.purpose: Identify the originating webpage at the top of a website-share or save dialog.
use_when: share/save dialogs initiated from the browser
modals that need to show URL provenance
dont_use_when: the share is not from a browser context — use dialog title/value

dialog.browser-top-bar.description: Pill-radius (48dp) translucent dark address bar header for the in-app browser modal showing URL hostname.
dialog.browser-top-bar.purpose: Header chrome for share-target and browser-style modals — identifies the source URL with minimal weight.
use_when: share-target sheets that show a webpage URL
in-app browser confirmation modals
dont_use_when: regular app screens — use appbar

dialog.icon-grid-box.description: 24dp-radius translucent dark grid (380x280dp) hosting share-target apps as 4-column icon+label cells.
dialog.icon-grid-box.purpose: Picker surface for share-targets / app selection inside a system dialog.
use_when: presenting a share sheet or app chooser
8+ targets need to be browsable in a tight modal
dont_use_when: fewer than 5 targets fit linearly — use list-item rows

dialog.page-indicator.description: Row of 3-radius pill dots (~6dp tall), 30% white, indicating multiple steps/pages in a wizard or paged dialog.
dialog.page-indicator.purpose: Show how many pages a multi-step modal/wizard has and where the user is currently.
use_when: wizards, onboarding modals, paged dialogs
dont_use_when: single-page dialogs

dialog.nav-gesture-bar.description: Short horizontal bar (~144x4dp), 2dp radius, 60% white — the home gesture-bar handle at the bottom safe area.
dialog.nav-gesture-bar.purpose: Render the system home/gesture-bar handle inside layouts to reserve and visualize the bottom safe area.
use_when: modeling any full-screen surface where gesture nav is active
dont_use_when: device runs three-button nav — use container.nav-buttons-light

system.dim-overlay.description: Full-screen scrim (rgba 0,0,0,0.2) with a 40dp outer radius — sits beneath modal dialog/sheet content.
system.dim-overlay.purpose: Visually de-emphasize the underlying screen and trap touch events for modal flows.
use_when: presenting a system dialog or sheet that must take focus
dimming areas outside an Edit window per One UI guidelines
dont_use_when: the surface is non-modal (snackbar, action toast)

search.dark.description: Pill-shaped 58dp search field, near-black (#17171A) fill, 30dp radius — sized to a full-width 412dp module.
search.dark.purpose: Primary search affordance for dark-themed app shells (Samsung Internet dark, Gallery dark, etc.).
use_when: the host shell is dark themed
search is the primary affordance at the top of a list/browse screen
dont_use_when: the shell is light — use search.light
the search invokes Galaxy AI — use search.galaxy-ai

search.light.description: Pill-shaped 58dp search field, near-white (#FCFCFF) fill, 30dp radius — sized to a full-width 412dp module.
search.light.purpose: Primary search affordance for light-themed app shells.
use_when: the host shell is light themed
search is the primary affordance at the top of a list/browse screen
dont_use_when: the shell is dark — use search.dark
the search invokes Galaxy AI — use search.galaxy-ai-light

search.galaxy-ai.description: Pill-shaped 58dp search field on a dark blue-to-grey gradient with a blue-to-teal AI gradient text fill and soft glow shadow.
search.galaxy-ai.purpose: Branded search entry point that signals Galaxy AI assistance — natural-language, summarization, or generative search.
use_when: the dark-themed surface needs to advertise AI search capability
the user is asking a question vs. typing a keyword
dont_use_when: the search runs purely lexical matching — use search.dark
shell is light themed — use search.galaxy-ai-light

search.galaxy-ai-light.description: Pill-shaped 58dp search field on a deep-blue-to-light-grey gradient with blue-to-teal AI gradient text fill and soft glow shadow.
search.galaxy-ai-light.purpose: Light-theme variant of Galaxy AI search — same intent as search.galaxy-ai but designed for light shells.
use_when: the light-themed surface needs to advertise AI search capability
dont_use_when: the search runs purely lexical matching — use search.light
shell is dark themed — use search.galaxy-ai

button.dark.description: Wide pill-shaped button (~406x74dp), 28dp radius, near-black surface with light-on-dark label — full bleed across content width.
button.dark.purpose: Confirm/secondary action on dark surfaces (system dialogs, dark app shell footers).
use_when: the host surface is dark and the button must read as elevated chrome
needing a full-width confirm-style action
dont_use_when: the surface is light — use button.light
the action is primary — use button.accent

button.light.description: Wide pill-shaped button (~406x74dp), 28dp radius, near-white surface with dark-on-light label — full bleed across content width.
button.light.purpose: Confirm/secondary action on light surfaces, mirroring button.dark for theme parity.
use_when: the host surface is light and the button must read as elevated chrome
needing a full-width confirm-style action in light theme
dont_use_when: the surface is dark — use button.dark
the action is primary — use button.accent

button.accent.description: Wide pill-shaped button (~406x74dp), 28dp radius, saturated Samsung blue (#387AFF) fill with white label.
button.accent.purpose: Primary CTA — the highest-priority action on the screen, visually committing the user to a result (Continue, Save, Pay, etc.).
use_when: presenting the destination action of a flow
exactly one action must dominate the surface
dont_use_when: more than one action competes — promote only one to accent
the action is destructive — pair with a flat cancel and use specific destructive styling if available

button.galaxy-ai.description: Wide pill button (~406x74dp), 28dp radius, blue-to-teal gradient fill signalling a Galaxy AI feature.
button.galaxy-ai.purpose: Primary CTA when the action invokes Galaxy AI (summarize, translate, generate) — used to brand AI-powered results.
use_when: the action triggers an on-device or cloud AI feature
users should recognize the action as an AI capability
dont_use_when: the action is not AI-related — use button.accent instead

button.header-small.description: Compact pill button (~90x36dp), 28dp radius, dark fill with 14sp label — sized to sit inline with header text.
button.header-small.purpose: Inline header action (e.g., 'Edit', 'See all', 'Done') anchored to a section title or app-bar trailing slot.
use_when: an action is tied to a specific header/section, not the whole screen
space is limited and a full-width button would overpower the header
dont_use_when: the action is the screen-level CTA — promote to button.accent

container.app-shell-dark.description: Full-screen dark (#010102) shell, 16dp outer radius — the root container for any dark-themed app screen.
container.app-shell-dark.purpose: Provide a single coherent dark canvas for the entire app screen, framing children consistently.
use_when: the app uses Dark theme as the host shell
the screen needs the corner-rounded container treatment
dont_use_when: the app is light themed — use container.app-shell-light

container.app-shell-light.description: Full-screen light (#F1F1F3) shell with a 32dp outer radius — the root container for any light-themed app screen.
container.app-shell-light.purpose: Provide a single coherent light canvas for the entire app screen, framing children consistently.
use_when: the app uses Light theme as the host shell
dont_use_when: the app is dark themed — use container.app-shell-dark

container.content-area.description: Inner 38dp-radius scrollable area inside the app shell, 10dp padding all around, inherits theme background.
container.content-area.purpose: Hold the scrollable body of an app screen between header chrome and bottom safe area.
use_when: every app screen — children stack vertically inside the shell
dont_use_when: rendering a glanceable widget — use widget-small or now-bar

container.header.description: Tall (~240dp) transparent header zone with a giant 36sp display title and 14sp supporting text — sits at the top of an app screen.
container.header.purpose: Expandable app-bar header that gives a screen its identity and supports collapsing into a regular appbar on scroll.
use_when: first-depth screens that benefit from a hero title (Settings, Inbox, Browse)
expandable app-bar pattern as documented in One UI
dont_use_when: second-depth screens — keep app-bar collapsed
landscape/full-screen views — expandable bar is not supported

container.status-bar-app.description: App-shell-aware top safe-area strip (~412x44dp), transparent — composes time/icons inside an app's framing.
container.status-bar-app.purpose: Reserve and align the top safe-area inside an app shell (Samsung style: time left, icons right).
use_when: designing inside an app shell where the system status bar is rendered as a content child
dont_use_when: the global system bar is already provided by the platform — use status-bar

container.nav-gestures-dark.description: Bottom safe-area strip (~412x24dp) housing the gesture-bar handle on dark themes — transparent background.
container.nav-gestures-dark.purpose: Reserve the bottom gesture-bar safe area in dark-themed app shells without rendering nav buttons.
use_when: the device runs in gesture nav mode and the host shell is dark
dont_use_when: device uses three-button nav — use container.nav-buttons-light

container.nav-buttons-light.description: Bottom safe-area strip (~412x56dp) holding the three-button navigation (Recents / Home / Back) on light themes.
container.nav-buttons-light.purpose: Render the legacy three-button nav for users who disabled gestures, in light-themed shells.
use_when: the device runs in three-button nav mode and the host shell is light
dont_use_when: device uses gesture nav — use container.nav-gestures-dark

card.toggle-row.description: 28dp-radius dark (#17171A) card row, ~78dp tall, with title + supporting text on the left and a switch on the right.
card.toggle-row.purpose: Settings entry that combines a label, helper copy and a toggle in one tappable row — dark theme.
use_when: settings rows that benefit from helper text alongside a toggle
dark-themed app shells
dont_use_when: light shell — use card.toggle-row-light
no helper text needed and the row is dense — use list-item + switch

card.toggle-row-light.description: 28dp-radius near-white (#FCFCFF) card row, ~78dp tall, with title + supporting text and a switch — light theme.
card.toggle-row-light.purpose: Settings entry that combines a label, helper copy and a toggle in one tappable row — light theme.
use_when: settings rows that benefit from helper text alongside a toggle
light-themed app shells
dont_use_when: dark shell — use card.toggle-row

card.menu-item.description: 28dp-radius card row (~391x64dp) inheriting theme background, single 18sp title — pure label, no helper text or trailing control.
card.menu-item.purpose: Single-line menu entry inside a settings or chooser group — title-only.
use_when: list of single-purpose menu entries with no auxiliary content
stacked inside card.stacked-group
dont_use_when: the row needs helper text — use card.menu-item-body
the row needs trailing chevron navigation — use card.chevron-nav

card.menu-item-body.description: 28dp-radius card row (~391x90dp) with an 18sp title and a 14sp body line beneath it — inherits theme.
card.menu-item-body.purpose: Menu entry that needs a description line under the title for clarity (privacy explanations, multi-step settings).
use_when: a menu entry's purpose isn't obvious from the title alone
the row should still feel like a list entry
dont_use_when: the description is too long — promote to card.subheading
no description is needed — use card.menu-item

card.subheading.description: Tall (~391x110dp) 28dp-radius card with a heading title and multi-line description body — non-interactive.
card.subheading.purpose: Section subheader card explaining a group of settings or controls that follow it.
use_when: introducing a settings group with multi-line guidance
providing context above a stacked group of menu rows
dont_use_when: the row is interactive — use card.menu-item-body
the section needs only a one-line label — use a primitive section label

card.chevron-nav.description: 28dp-radius card row (~412x64dp), 18sp title with a trailing chevron — entire surface is the tap target.
card.chevron-nav.purpose: Drill-down navigation entry that opens a deeper screen (sub-settings, account, profile).
use_when: a row leads to another screen rather than toggling state
users need to recognize 'tap here to dive in'
dont_use_when: the row toggles a setting — use card.toggle-row
the row selects a value — use card.radio

card.radio.description: Full-width 28dp-radius card row, 64dp tall, with a leading title and a trailing radio dot — entire row is the tap target.
card.radio.purpose: Single-select option presented as a substantial card — used when the choice deserves more than a row in a list.
use_when: presenting a small set of mutually-exclusive options inside a settings or dialog flow
each option benefits from generous touch area
dont_use_when: the choice list is dense — use radio inside list-item
selections are not exclusive — use checkbox / chip

card.stacked-group.description: Single 28dp-radius outer shell that visually merges multiple card rows — only first/last children keep rounded corners, dividers between them.
card.stacked-group.purpose: Group consecutive card rows (toggle, menu, chevron) into one cohesive section without per-row gaps.
use_when: settings screens grouping related rows under one header
you want the look-and-feel of a single bordered group
dont_use_when: rows are unrelated and should feel separate

switch.modern.description: Compact 40x22dp pill switch using current Samsung blue (#0381FE) on, neutral grey off — modern One UI styling.
switch.modern.purpose: Theme-aligned drop-in replacement for the legacy switch — same intent, refreshed visuals for newer surfaces.
use_when: settings rows on modern One UI surfaces
matching the latest Samsung visual language
dont_use_when: preserving legacy styling intentionally — use switch

system.dialog-surface.description: 32dp-radius translucent dark glass surface (~415x500dp) for system-level dialogs — title 18sp, body 14sp.
system.dialog-surface.purpose: Container for full system dialogs (permissions, share targets, account chooser) layered above app content.
use_when: presenting a system-level dialog over apps
share-targets, permission requests, account chooser
dont_use_when: in-app confirmations — use dialog

vocabulary.note: STEP 3 (component_selector) must select component_type ONLY from semantic_allowed_types. The broader allowed_types list includes UI primitives used by the composer/renderer but is NOT exposed to the planner.
